publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2020-11-06 18:08:29 -0500.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.physdesc: Correspondence, Financial Records, Legal Documents, Research Materials
did.abstract: Correspondence, grocery and general merchandise daybooks, cashbooks, customers' ledgers, inventories, order books, checkbooks and other accounts mainly of Hammond Brothers, the Hammond Store, and [E.J.] Barker and [Wallace] Wyman, successors to the Hammonds; also records relating to company railroad employees, canal and lake steamer shipping accounts, and journals of the Crown Point Iron Company and the Sugar Hill Company.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Members of the Hammond family owned and operated the Crown Point Iron Company, the Sugar Hill Company, and the Hammond Store in various configurations. The Crown Point Iron Company operated under various names, including Penfield and [Timothy] Taft, Penfield and [Eleazor] Harwood, Penfield and Hammond, and John and Thomas Hammond. Allen Penfield and Charles Franklin Hammond were brothers-in-law.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: The bulk of these papers consist of grocery and general merchandise daybooks, cashbooks, customers' ledgers, inventories, order books, checkbooks and other accounts (80 v., 1851-1854, 1872-1916) mainly of Hammond Brothers (John and Thomas), the Hammond Store, and [E.J.] Barker and [Wallace] Wyman, successors (1871) to the Hammonds; also cashbooks, payroll records and board bills for furnace, furnace store, and company railroad employees, canal and lake steamer shipping accounts, and journals (7 v., 1858-1880) of the Crown Point Iron Company; accounts for the furnace company store, which include daybooks, customers' ledgers, and a cashbook (11 v., 1874-1886); miscellaneous accounts (11 v., 1847-1889) of Hammond and Company, Charles Franklin Hammond, the Sugar Hill Company, and others; bankbooks (10 v., 1835-1866) and checkbook stubs (13 v., 1855-1883), covering accounts which C. F. Hammond and Hammond and Company had with banks in Whitehall and Troy; and one daybook of Allen Penfield (1845-1861). In addition to bound account books, the papers also include correspondence, financial statements, orders, invoices, vouchers, board and freight bills, boat operation receipts, general store bills and receipts, and memoranda notebooks. Essex County.

emph.ref: Series I. Hammond Family Business Records

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Hammond family business papers, #2139. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

controlaccess.subject: Railroads -- New York (State) -- Crown Point.
Business records -- New York (State) -- Crown Point.

Grocery trade -- New York (State) -- Crown Point
General stores -- New York (State) -- Crown Point
controlaccess.corpname: Crown Point Iron Company (Crown Point, N.Y.)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Hammond Store and Crown Point Iron Company

did.unittitle: Market Bank Vouchers and bank book

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Similar types of document to rest of box

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Similar type of documents in Box 4; bills for frei8ght included

did.unittitle: Crown Point Iron Co. Financial Statements
Merchants and Mechanics Bank check stubs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Same type as rest of box, including Freight and Clay Statements for 1871

did.unittitle: Union Bank Vouchers CF Hammond Account
Invoices of Crockery - Stationary Wall Paper and Co
Merchants and Mechanics Bank Certificates of Deposit

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1880, 1892-1893, 1902-1903 represent only some of the dates

did.unittitle: Lumber Iron Boat receipt & CF Hammond Inspectors Bills
Hammond Co Union Bank Vouchers and H & Co. balances
LR Berry Store Crown Point NY

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Daily store transactions with purchases itemized under customers' names.

did.unittitle: Barker & Wyman Crown Point NY Store Day Book
John Sabombard P. Marcus Store Day Book

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Daily store transactions with purchases itemized under customers' names.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Daily record of purchases to be delivered

did.unittitle: Reynolds Consecutive Book Odd Store Day Book
WE Petty Odd Store Day Book
Barker and Co Odd Store Day Book
LR Berry Crown Point NY Odd Store Day Book

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Broadway and Maiden Lane Brick Store on the Corner

did.unittitle: RR Book Odd Store Day Book

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Entries transferred from store day book.

did.unittitle: Master Customer Ledgers No. 3 - Furnace Store
Checks National Bank Troy - Check Books / Stubs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two filled out checks on JW Wyman. Banker

did.unittitle: WB Viall Crown Point Essex Co. Market Barker and Wyman Dealers - Order Book

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: in dry goods, groceries, hats, caps, boots, shoes, iron, steel, nails etc.

did.unittitle: J & T Hammond Store - Cash Book

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Seems to be record of customer payment to company

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Seems to be record of cost of stock. Dry goods & notions, crockery and stoneware, groceries etc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Possibly used by both the Hammond Store and the Crown Point Iron Company or impossible to determine or miscellaneous.

did.unittitle: Bill Book Antoine Delona District 3 Rivers River Du Loup C E

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Seems to be concerned with banks and checks. Fragments of other books inserted

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Probably used by CF Hammond as a highway commissioner

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Hours, daily rates and monthly wages

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Better be preserved as a memorandum TH, pages all torn out

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Often on different banks for parallel periods. Probably used for both store and iron company.
